control._sprint_114_note: a_max/a_decel/v_body_max/j_max/yaw_jerk_max added 2026-07-20 (sprint 114 ticket 002, config-as-truth completion): gen_boot_config.py no longer carries these as Python-side *_DEFAULT fallback constants -- every value below is seeded with the exact former default, value-preserving (verified byte-identical boot_config.cpp regen). togov is touched only to stay schema-complete, not bench-retuned (togov mecanum bench characterization is out of sprint 114's scope). This note originally also covered a dozen sibling control.* keys (heading/lead/distance/model-tau/outer-loop/turn-rate) -- ALL deleted (119-003, config-attic sweep): no living consumer survived 115-003's motion-stack excision.
control._shaper_note: decel-into-the-goal campaign (2026-07-22): a_max/a_decel/j_max/yaw_jerk_max read again by gen_boot_config.py's shaper_config_for_config() (Motion::VelocityShaper) -- see tovez.json's own _shaper_note for the full history/derivation. alpha_max/alpha_decel are new this campaign; 7.0rad/s^2 matches tovez.json's own value/derivation, touched only to stay schema-complete (togov mecanum bench characterization remains out of scope, same posture as this file's own _sprint_114_note above). j_max/yaw_jerk_max power the jerk-limited S-curve stage (same day) -- values unchanged.

estimator._estimator_note: 117 (predict-to-now estimator v1): weight_heading_otos/weight_omega_otos committed 0.0 -- stakeholder's encoder-only-v1 decision, OTOS fusion is wired but not yet trusted with any nonzero weight. staleness_ms=60.0 is 3x Devices::Otos::kReadPeriod (20ms): generous headroom over the OTOS chip's own read cadence, small enough to exclude a genuinely stale reading before the next cycle's fresh one arrives. togov is touched only to stay schema-complete, matching this file's own control._sprint_114_note precedent. (118 ticket 004, land-at-zero-completion-delete-stop-lead.md: this section's own former fourth key -- a boot-time anticipation-lead constant -- is DELETED; the mechanism it fed no longer exists, see App::MoveQueue::tick()'s own doc comment for the land-at-zero completion predicate that replaces it.)
